description: Running log of changes since the user-facing documentation was last updated. Consumed by the documentation-update tool. Clear the 'changes' array (on request) once the docs have been regenerated.

changes.title: Keyboard stays up the whole time About Me is open
changes.summary: Opening the About Me tab in Settings now shows the on-screen keyboard right away and keeps it displayed until you tap Done, instead of it only appearing when you tap into a text box.
details: When you open About Me (the About Me tab in Settings), the on-screen keyboard appears immediately in your chosen dock position.
It stays displayed the entire time About Me is open - on the topic list as well as inside a topic - so it no longer appears and disappears as you move between screens and entries.
Tapping into an answer box lets you type as before; the keyboard simply never goes away while About Me is open.
Tapping Done closes About Me and takes the keyboard down (and returns you to Settings).
Only applies when 'Keyboard for typing' is set to the on-screen keyboard; with a physical keyboard nothing changes.

changes.title: Keyboard separation setting
changes.summary: A new Settings control sets the gap between the keyboard / Express Panel and the rest of the screen, separate from the gap between buttons.
details: New slider 'Keyboard separation' in Settings -> Speech & Input (below 'Minimum gap'), with - and + step buttons like the other sizing sliders.
Controls the space between the dock (the keyboard / Express Panel area) and the rest of the UI; it is independent of the 'Button spacing' (inter-button) gap.
It moves only the other content away from the keyboard - the keyboard keeps its position, so changing it does NOT move the keyguard holes and the keyguard does not need to be re-cut.
Applies on every screen that reserves the dock area: the conversation screen, the Settings panel, and the About Me screen, for both the side dock and the bottom dock.
Default is 0 (flush, the previous look). Range 0 to about 4rem.
Deliberately NOT reset by the 'Reset buttons and gaps to default' button - keyboard separation is an independent layout preference.
